preK.definition: A clue is a little hint that helps you find something, like a tiny cookie sprinkle can help you find cookies!
preK.example: That tiny cookie sprinkle on the floor is a clue that yummy cookies might be in the cabinet!
preK.tryIt: What do you think this colorful sprinkle is a clue for?

K.definition: A clue is a little hint that helps you find or guess something, like the tiny sprinkle helping you guess what's in the cabinet.
K.example: The tiny sprinkle on the floor was a perfect clue to guess there were cookies in the cabinet!
K.tryIt: What other little clues might you find near the cabinet that could tell you what's inside?

G1.definition: A clue is a little hint that helps you find something, like a tiny cookie sprinkle on the floor.
G1.example: The tiny cookie sprinkle on the floor was a clue about where yummy treats might be hiding!
G1.tryIt: If you found a tiny toy block on the floor, what would that be a clue about?

preK.definition: It's a little sad feeling you get when something you hoped for doesn't happen.
preK.example: I felt a little disappointment when my friend couldn't come over to play today.
preK.tryIt: What might make you feel a little disappointment?

K.definition: It's when you feel a little sad because something you really hoped for didn't happen the way you wanted.
K.example: Lily felt **disappointment** when her balloon popped before she could show it to her friends.
K.tryIt: Think of a time you were really hoping for a sunny day to play outside, but it rained instead. That's a feeling of disappointment!

G1.definition: It's a sad feeling you get when something you hoped for or really wanted to happen doesn't happen.
G1.example: I felt disappointment when it started to rain and we couldn't go to the park.
G1.tryIt: What would make you feel disappointment? Maybe if your friend couldn't come over to play?

preK.definition: A dream is like pictures you see in your head when you are sleeping.
preK.example: The child is having a happy dream about a fluffy bunny playing gently with a colorful ball.
preK.tryIt: What do you like to dream about when you are sleeping?

K.definition: A dream is like a fun picture or story you see in your head when you are sleeping, just like the bunny playing above the child's head!
K.example: The child is having a happy dream about a fluffy bunny playing with a colorful ball.
K.tryIt: Can you point to the fluffy bunny that is part of the child's dream?

G1.definition: A dream is like a picture story you see in your head when you are sleeping, just like the bunny playing above the child.
G1.example: The sleeping child is having a happy dream about a fluffy bunny playing with a colorful ball.
G1.tryIt: What do you think the friendly bunny in the child's dream will do next?

preK.definition: Impressive means something is really, really good and makes you say 'Wow!' like that super tall block tower!
preK.example: The friendly bunny thinks your tall block tower is very impressive!
preK.tryIt: Can you make an 'impressive' face like the bunny looking at the tower?

K.definition: Impressive means something is so good or big it makes you say 'Wow!' just like that super tall block tower.
K.example: Look! The squirrel thinks your tall block tower is so impressive!
K.tryIt: Can you point to something else in this picture that looks impressive?

G1.definition: Impressive means something is so good or big that it makes you say, "Wow!" Just like the tall block tower!
G1.example: The tall block tower was so impressive that the rabbit looked up with wide, happy eyes.
G1.tryIt: What makes the tall block tower look so impressive?

preK.definition: A mystery is when you don't know who did something, like who ate the cookie!
preK.example: The child in the kitchen is trying to solve the mystery of the missing cookie.
preK.tryIt: Look at the picture! Can you help solve the mystery? Who do you think ate the yummy cookie?

K.definition: A mystery is something you don't know the answer to, like who ate the cookie!
K.example: The child looked at the half-eaten cookie and thought, 'It's a mystery who ate it!'
K.tryIt: Look at the picture! Can you guess who might know the secret about the missing cookie?

G1.definition: A mystery is a puzzle, like when you don't know who ate the cookie!
G1.example: The child looked at the half-eaten cookie, and it was a big mystery who took a bite!
G1.tryIt: Look at the picture. Can you guess who ate the cookie? You're trying to solve the mystery!

preK.definition: Respect means being kind and careful with something important, like your plant.
preK.example: The child shows respect for the plant by carefully pouring water, helping it grow.
preK.tryIt: How is the child in the picture showing respect for the plant?

K.definition: Respect means you are kind and careful with something, like when you water your plant so it can grow.
K.example: The child shows respect for the little plant by gently giving it water.
K.tryIt: If you had a little plant, how would you water it gently to show respect?

G1.definition: Respect means showing you care about something or someone. Like when you carefully water a plant.
G1.example: The child shows respect by gently pouring water so the little plant can drink.
G1.tryIt: What is something at your home that you can show respect to, just like you show respect to the plant?

preK.definition: Responsibility is when you take care of something important, like putting your toys in the bin.
preK.example: The child shows responsibility by gently placing their teddy bear back in the toy bin.
preK.tryIt: What toy can you show responsibility for by putting it in your toy bin?

K.definition: Responsibility means it's your job to take care of things, like putting your toys in their bin.
K.example: When you gently put your favorite toy into the bin, you are showing responsibility for your things.
K.tryIt: What is a responsibility you have with your toys?

G1.definition: Responsibility is when you do what you are supposed to do, like putting your toys in the bin.
G1.example: Putting your teddy bear neatly in the toy bin shows great responsibility.
G1.tryIt: What is a responsibility you have for your toys at home?

preK.definition: Safety means being careful so you don't get hurt. Like wearing your helmet!
preK.example: Wearing a helmet helps keep you safe when you ride your scooter or tricycle.
preK.tryIt: What helps keep the child's head safe when they ride the scooter?

K.definition: Safety means staying safe and not getting hurt. Wearing your helmet helps with safety!
K.example: Putting on a helmet before riding the scooter is good safety.
K.tryIt: Why do you think the child is wearing a helmet?

G1.definition: Safety means being careful so you don't get hurt, like wearing your helmet.
G1.example: The child puts on their helmet for safety before riding the scooter.
G1.tryIt: Why do you think wearing a helmet is a safe thing to do when riding a scooter or tricycle?
